response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 15+ years of genuine engineering breadth — high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby, full-stack product work, embedded systems, and recent AI-agent tooling — and his language portfolio overlaps well with the role's requirements. However, the Principal Growth/Experimentation position demands deep, specialized expertise in A/B testing frameworks, experiment assignment and targeting, statistical bias detection, telemetry pipeline ownership, and rollout-risk management. None of these appear anywhere in the resume. His career arc is that of a skilled full-stack craftsman and open-source contributor, not a growth-platform architect. Without evidence of the core specializations, he falls significantly short of the IC6 bar for this domain.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and low-latency design
Broad language fluency across C, C++, TypeScript/JavaScript, Java, and C# aligns directly with the role's listed language requirements
Consistent engineering-craft discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code reviews, and community mentoring — signals production-quality habits
concerns: Zero evidence of experimentation platforms, A/B testing, feature flagging, experiment assignment/targeting, or rollout-risk management — the explicit core of this role
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure experience visible; no mention of instrumentation pipelines, statistical analysis, or data-driven decision systems
No demonstrated Principal- or IC6-level cross-team technical leadership, growth engineering ownership, or systemic platform work enabling multiple teams

key_factors.factor: No telemetry, metrics, or statistical analysis background
